name: controllable-state
title: Controllable State
description: Generic controlled/uncontrolled state hook. Manages value, defaultValue, and onChange for any component that supports both modes — like React's built-in input pattern, but reusable.

docs.description: Generic controlled/uncontrolled state hook. Manages value, defaultValue, and onChange for any component that supports both modes — like React's built-in input pattern, but reusable.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Controlled value. When provided, the component is in controlled mode.

parameters.name: onChange
parameters.description: Called when the value changes, in both controlled and uncontrolled modes.

returns.description: Tuple of [currentValue, setValue, isControlled]. The setter accepts both direct values and updater functions.

properties.name: currentValue
properties.description: The current value, whether controlled or uncontrolled.

properties.name: setValue
properties.description: State setter that works in both modes. Supports functional updates.

properties.name: isControlled
properties.description: Whether the consumer is driving the value externally.

notes.title: When to Use
notes.content: Use when building a component that should work both as controlled (value + onChange) and uncontrolled (defaultValue). It handles the state switching logic so the component doesn't need to.

notes.title: Return Value
notes.content: The isControlled flag tells you whether the consumer is driving the value externally. Use it for conditional logic like preventing internal state updates.

content.text: border border-neutral-700 bg-neutral-900 px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-neutral-100
